description: Analyze the assembly of the Cholera Toxin hexamer (PDB: 1XTC). First, visually separate the catalytic A-subunit (chain A) from the B-pentamer ring (chains B-F). Second, highlight the primary receptor-binding residue, Tryptophan 88 (Trp88), on all B-subunits. Third, quantitatively map the protein-protein interface by identifying all B-pentamer residues that are within 4.0 Angstroms of the A-subunit's anchoring helix (defined as residues 195 to 240 of chain A). Render a publication-quality figure of the complex to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/cholera_ab5.png. Finally, write a structural report to ~/PyMOL_Data/cholera_interface_report.txt containing: the chain IDs of the complex, the total count of Trp88 residues present, and a list of the interface B-pentamer residues formatted as 'Chain:Resi' (e.g., 'B:72', 'C:75').
